schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare
schemes.short_description: Direct income support for farmers, providing ₹6,000 annually in three installments.
schemes.detailed_description: A central sector scheme providing ₹6,000 per year to all landholding farmer families across the country to help meet their financial needs for procuring various inputs related to agriculture and allied activities.
key_benefits: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to bank accounts
Paid in three equal installments of ₹2,000 each
other_conditions: Must have cultivable landholding in their name
Excludes institutional landholders, professionals (doctors, engineers), and income tax payers

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Used for identity verification and DBT seeding.
Self-certified copies of land ownership documents/records.
Copy of the first page showing account number and IFSC code.

application_steps_online: Visit the official PM-KISAN portal.
Click on 'New Farmer Registration' under the Farmers Corner.
Enter Aadhaar Number and select State/District.
Fill in personal, bank, and land details.
Upload scanned documents and submit the application.
Save the reference number for tracking.
application_steps_offline: Visit the local Agriculture Office or Nodal Officer.
Collect and fill out the physical registration form.
Attach copies of Aadhaar, bank passbook, and land records.
Submit to the officer for verification and digital entry.

submission_locations.notes: Visit local Patwari or Talati for land record verification.

schemes.processing_time: Varies (subject to state nodal officer approval)

schemes.scheme_name: Ayushman Bharat Pradhan Mantri Jan Arogya Yojana (AB-PMJAY)
schemes.short_description: Provides cashless health insurance coverage of up to ₹5 Lakh per family per year.
schemes.detailed_description: The world's largest health assurance scheme providing a health cover of ₹5,00,000 per family per year for secondary and tertiary care hospitalization to poor and vulnerable families based on SECC 2011 criteria.
key_benefits: ₹5,00,000 cashless cover per family per year
Covers pre-existing diseases from day one
Covers 3 days pre-hospitalization and 15 days post-hospitalization
No restriction on family size, age, or gender
schemes.age_criteria: No age limit (Includes specific 70+ senior citizen coverage)
schemes.occupation_criteria: Manual laborers, destitute, specific unorganized sectors

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Needed to verify family structure and eligibility list.

application_steps_online: Visit the 'Am I Eligible' portal.
Enter mobile number and Captcha to receive OTP.
Search by Name, Ration Card number, or HHD number.
If name appears, proceed to verify e-KYC using Aadhaar.
Download Ayushman Card after approval.
application_steps_offline: Visit an empanelled government or private hospital.
Contact the 'Ayushman Mitra' help desk.
Provide Aadhaar and Ration Card for eligibility check.
Perform biometric authentication on-site.
Receive Ayushman Golden Card.

submission_locations.notes: Can facilitate card generation for a nominal fee.

schemes.short_description: A government-backed savings scheme to secure the financial future of a girl child.
schemes.detailed_description: Part of the 'Beti Bachao Beti Padhao' campaign, this scheme allows parents to open a high-interest, tax-free savings account for a girl child under 10 years of age to fund her future education and marriage expenses.
schemes.age_criteria: Girl child must be below 10 years at account opening
other_conditions: Maximum of two accounts per family (one for each girl), with exceptions for twins/triplets
Minimum deposit of ₹250 per financial year, maximum of ₹1.5 Lakh

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, or Passport.
Utility bill or Voter ID of parent.

application_steps_offline: Visit a Post Office or authorized Commercial Bank.
Fill out the SSY account opening form.
Attach photographs of the parent and child.
Submit the form along with initial deposit (min ₹250).
Collect the SSY Passbook.

submission_locations.notes: Available in all India Post branches.
Select private banks like ICICI/HDFC also authorized.

common_rejection_reasons: Name discrepancies on the birth certificate
Attempting to open an account for a child older than 10 years
Attempting to open more than two accounts per family (without twins)

schemes.short_description: Provides collateral-free loans to non-farm micro and small enterprises.
schemes.detailed_description: MUDRA provides refinance support to banks, NBFCs, and MFIs to lend up to ₹10 lakh (and up to ₹20 lakh under Tarun Plus) to micro-units in the manufacturing, processing, trading, or service sectors.
other_conditions: Applicant must not be a defaulter to any bank or financial institution
Must possess necessary skills/experience for the proposed business activity

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Electricity bill, Rent agreement, or Business license.
Basic business plan for Kishor/Tarun categories.

application_steps_online: Visit Jan Samarth portal.
Select 'Business Activity Loan'.
Answer eligibility questions to find Mudra scheme.
Choose the lender bank.
Fill the online application and upload documents.
Receive digital approval/in-principle offer.
application_steps_offline: Visit any commercial bank, Regional Rural Bank, or NBFC.
Ask for a Mudra Loan application form (Shishu/Kishor/Tarun).
Submit the form along with proof of identity and business plan.
The bank will process the loan based on internal diligence.
schemes.processing_time: 7 to 14 days depending on bank diligence
common_rejection_reasons: Poor credit score or history of loan defaults
Low or non-viable business project plan
Lack of required licenses to operate the business

schemes.short_description: Voluntary old age income security scheme for unorganized sector workers.
schemes.detailed_description: A guaranteed pension scheme administered by PFRDA that provides a minimum assured pension ranging from ₹1,000 to ₹5,000 per month post the age of 60, based on subscriber contributions. Auto-debit facilities ensure seamless premium collection.
schemes.income_criteria: Must not be an income tax payer (effective Oct 1, 2022)
other_conditions: Must possess an active savings bank account or post office savings account
Consent for auto-debit of premiums is mandatory
application_steps_online: Visit your bank's net banking portal or eNPS portal.
Navigate to 'Social Security Schemes' or 'APY'.
Verify Aadhaar details.
Choose pension amount (1k to 5k) and contribution frequency.
E-sign the auto-debit mandate.
application_steps_offline: Visit the bank branch where you have a savings account.
Fill out the APY Registration Form.
Provide Aadhaar and mobile number.
Ensure the bank official enables the auto-debit feature.

submission_locations.notes: Most public and private banks offer this service.

common_rejection_reasons: Applicant is over 40 years of age
Applicant is flagged as an income tax payer
Failed auto-debit setup due to inactive bank account
tips_for_successful_application: Ensure sufficient balance is maintained in the bank account to avoid auto-debit bounce penalties.
Join at age 18 to keep the monthly contribution as low as ₹42.

schemes.scheme_name: PM Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi (PM SVANidhi)
schemes.short_description: Micro-credit facility to help street vendors resume their livelihoods.
schemes.detailed_description: A special micro-credit facility facilitating collateral-free working capital loans starting at ₹10,000 for one year to street vendors. It incentivizes regular repayment with a 7% interest subsidy and cashback on digital transactions, unlocking higher loan limits (₹20,000 and ₹50,000) in subsequent tranches.
key_benefits: Collateral-free working capital loan up to ₹10,000 initially
7% per annum interest subsidy on timely repayment
Monthly cashback incentives for executing digital transactions

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Issued by Urban Local Bodies (ULB) or Letter of Recommendation (LoR).
Voter ID or PAN for additional verification.

application_steps_online: Visit the PM SVANidhi portal.
Enter Aadhaar-linked mobile number to get OTP.
Check eligibility by entering ULB or LoR details.
Fill the digital application form.
Select the preferred lender bank.
Submit and wait for SMS confirmation.
application_steps_offline: Approach a local Banking Correspondent (BC) or Municipal agent.
Fill out the physical loan application.
Provide Aadhaar and proof of vending status.
The agent will upload the details to the system.

submission_locations.notes: Banking Correspondents visit market areas for this.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
schemes.short_description: Provides clean cooking fuel (LPG) connections to women from poor households.
schemes.detailed_description: Aimed at reducing indoor air pollution and health hazards, PMUY provides a deposit-free LPG connection to women belonging to BPL households, SC/ST communities, Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) beneficiaries, and forest dwellers. Ujjwala 2.0 includes special provisions for migrant workers.
schemes.income_criteria: Poor household based on 14-point deprivation declaration or specific categories

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Proof of Identity for applicant and family.
Issued by State Govt showing family composition.

application_steps_online: Visit the official PMUY portal.
Choose the preferred LPG company (Indane, Bharat Gas, HP).
Fill in personal details and upload Aadhaar.
Enter Ration Card and family member details.
Submit and note the tracking ID.
application_steps_offline: Visit the nearest LPG distributor office.
Ask for a Ujjwala application form.
Submit the form with copies of Aadhaar, Ration Card, and Bank details.
The distributor will verify the physical premises for installation.

submission_locations.notes: Helpful for those without internet access.
Direct submission to the gas agency.

schemes.scheme_name: Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA)
schemes.short_description: Provides a legal guarantee for 100 days of wage employment in rural areas.
schemes.detailed_description: A landmark labor law and social security measure that aims to guarantee the 'right to work'. It ensures at least 100 days of unskilled manual labor in every financial year to adult members of any rural household.
schemes.financial_assistance: Daily wage transferred directly to bank account
other_conditions: Applicant must reside in a rural area
Must be willing to do unskilled manual work
application_steps_offline: Visit the Gram Panchayat office.
Submit an application for a Job Card.
The Panchayat will verify the household details and issue the Job Card.
Submit a formal demand for work to the Sarpanch or Gram Sevak.
schemes.processing_time: 15 days for Job Card and work allocation

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises
schemes.short_description: End-to-end support for traditional artisans and craftspeople.
schemes.detailed_description: A holistic scheme providing PM Vishwakarma ID cards, skill upgradation (with stipends), toolkit incentives of ₹15,000, and collateral-free enterprise development loans up to ₹3 lakh at a concessional 5% interest rate to artisans engaged in 18 specific traditional trades.
schemes.age_criteria: Minimum 18 years on the date of registration
schemes.occupation_criteria: Artisans/craftspeople in 18 traditional trades (e.g., carpenters, blacksmiths, goldsmiths)
application_steps_offline: Visit nearest CSC Center.
Complete biometric-based Aadhaar authentication.
Fill the Vishwakarma registration form.
Undergo verification by the Gram Panchayat or ULB level.
Receive Vishwakarma Certificate and ID Card.

schemes.ministry_department: Ministry of Women and Child Development
schemes.short_description: Maternity benefit scheme providing cash incentives to pregnant and lactating women.
schemes.detailed_description: Provides a partial wage compensation to women for wage-loss during childbirth and childcare. It grants ₹5,000 for the first child and ₹6,000 for a second child (if female) to ensure adequate maternal nutrition and promote health-seeking behavior.
key_benefits: ₹5,000 for the first child transferred in two installments
₹6,000 single installment for a second child, provided the child is a girl
schemes.income_criteria: Net family income < ₹8 Lakh per annum, or BPL status
other_conditions: Must apply within 270 days of childbirth
Must possess an active Mother Child Protection (MCP) card

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Mother Child Protection card for tracking health stages.
Account where cash benefit will be credited.

application_steps_online: Visit PMMVY portal (PMMVY-CAS).
Login as a Citizen or Beneficiary.
Enter Aadhaar and Mobile number.
Fill Form 1-A/1-B based on child number.
Upload scans of MCP card and Aadhaar.
Submit to the respective field officer.
application_steps_offline: Visit the nearest Anganwadi Centre or ASHA worker.
Submit the physical registration form.
Provide copy of MCP card showing early registration and ANC checks.
ASHA worker will coordinate the data entry.

submission_locations.notes: Anganwadi workers assist in filling the forms.

schemes.processing_time: Varies (Disbursed in tranches aligned with pregnancy stages)

schemes.short_description: Affordable housing scheme for urban poor, providing financial assistance for home construction or purchase.
schemes.detailed_description: Implemented through four verticals (Beneficiary Led Construction, Affordable Housing in Partnership, Affordable Rental Housing, and Interest Subsidy Scheme), it provides central assistance to EWS, LIG, and MIG segments to ensure 'Housing for All'.
schemes.income_criteria: EWS: Up to ₹3L, LIG: ₹3L-₹6L, MIG-I: ₹6L-₹12L, MIG-II: ₹12L-₹18L
schemes.geographic_criteria: Statutory Towns and Notified Urban Areas

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Property tax receipt or utility bill.

application_steps_online: Visit PMAY-MIS website.
Click 'Citizen Assessment' and select 'Benefit under 3 components'.
Enter Aadhaar number.
Fill in personal, income, and bank details.
Save and print the Assessment ID.
application_steps_offline: Visit the local Municipal Corporation office.
Ask for a PMAY-U application form.
Submit the form with Aadhaar and income proof.
Wait for on-site verification by Municipal officers.
schemes.processing_time: Varies by PLI and Central Nodal Agency approval
faq: Can husband and wife apply separately? No, married couples are treated as one unit and will be rejected if both apply.

schemes.short_description: Financial assistance to rural families for constructing permanent (pucca) houses.
schemes.detailed_description: A rural housing program funded 60:40 by the Central and State governments, providing financial grants to houseless families and those living in dilapidated kutcha houses to build secure, permanent structures with basic amenities.
schemes.income_criteria: Based strictly on SECC 2011 deprivation parameters

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Swachh Bharat Mission registration number for toilet subsidy.

application_steps_online: Access the AwaasSoft portal.
Entry is usually made by the Gram Sevak/Official using data from SECC 2011.
Check name on the 'Waiting List' portal.
Upload geofenced photos of current house.
application_steps_offline: Submit details to the Gram Sabha.
Provide identity and bank documents.
Verification by Block Development Officer (BDO).
Geotagging of land/plot site.

submission_locations.notes: Gram Panchayat is the first point of contact.

schemes.processing_time: Dependent on Gram Sabha verification cycles
tips_for_successful_application: Verify your name on the official pmayg.nic.in list before investing time in the process.

schemes.short_description: Provides monthly financial aid of ₹1,500 to eligible women in Maharashtra.
schemes.detailed_description: A state-level DBT scheme designed to ensure the economic independence of women, improve their health and nutrition, and strengthen their decisive role in the family. It provides ₹1,500 per month directly to their bank accounts.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Proof of residence in Maharashtra (or Ration Card/Voter ID as alternative).
Showing family income less than 2.5 Lakh.

application_steps_online: Download the 'Nari Shakti Doot' App or visit the web portal.
Register using mobile number.
Upload scans of Aadhaar, Domicile, and Income proof.
Fill out self-declaration form.
Submit and track status online.
application_steps_offline: Visit nearest Anganwadi Centre or Setu Kendra.
Submit the physical application form.
Attach self-attested documents.
The Sevika will upload details into the portal.

submission_locations.notes: Anganwadi workers are authorized for doorstep help.

common_rejection_reasons: Family income exceeds the ₹2.5 Lakh threshold
Aadhaar is not seeded with the provided bank account

schemes.scheme_name: Reimbursement of Education Fees for Backward Class Students
schemes.short_description: Fee reimbursement scheme for SC/Neo-Buddhist students pursuing higher education in Maharashtra.
schemes.detailed_description: A 100% state-funded scheme designed to reduce the dropout rate by fully reimbursing education, tuition, and examination fees for students from Scheduled Castes and Neo-Buddhist categories enrolled in recognized post-matriculation courses.
schemes.income_criteria: Parents' annual income must be > ₹2.50 lakh (if less, apply for GoI Post-Matric)
other_conditions: Must be pursuing post-matric studies at recognized district-level institutions
application_steps_online: Login to MahaDBT portal.
Create profile with Aadhaar and academic history.
Select the scheme under Social Justice department.
Upload required scanned documents.
Submit and forward to Institute level for approval.
tips_for_successful_application: Coordinate with your college clerk to ensure your application is verified and forwarded to the district office promptly.

schemes.short_description: Additional financial top-up for PM-KISAN beneficiaries in Maharashtra.
schemes.detailed_description: Transfers an additional ₹6,000 annually in three equal installments to farmers in Maharashtra who are already verified and receiving funds under the central PM-KISAN scheme, doubling their total direct benefit.

REQUIRED_DOCUMENTS.description: Active status in central PM-Kisan portal is mandatory.

application_steps_online: Registration is automatic for farmers already enrolled in PM-KISAN.
Ensure your Bank Account is Aadhaar-seeded.
Update e-KYC on the PM-Kisan portal if pending.
Check status on the MahaIT or Maharashtra Agriculture portal.
